cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about when the father was laid off, what he did. The answer choices are A to D. Looking back at the story, when the father told his son Andrew that he was laid off, the son first thought he was joking. But then he noticed the father's expression and realized it was true. The story mentions that the father was a hard worker and took pride in his career. After losing his job, the father was home all day, searching for jobs on the computer. The son noticed how "down he seemed" and how it affected his self-esteem, even though he tried to be optimistic. So, the key points here are that the father was affected emotionally—felt down, his self-esteem was affected, and he was trying to stay optimistic. This suggests that he had a hard time accepting the layoff. Looking at the options: A) was angry with his boss – The story doesn't mention any anger towards the boss. The father just said he was laid off quietly, no indication of anger. B) didn't care about it at all – This is the opposite. The father clearly was affected, so B is not correct.
